Trash

Mrs. Pitts’ strength is that she listened to my concerns and made boundaries to protect both my assets and the well being of my family. Her years of experience allow her to make suggestions that are reasonable and effective. The legal process is a long process, but the end result worked everything out for the good of my family.

Aaaah Ms Beth Pitts or should I say Morgan Pitts Beth. She talks a good game but at the end of the day she mismanaged and prioritized me at the bottom as well.

Its kinda a funny because yes she did help me, but changed for the worse when we didn’t see eye to eye and tried to convince me no wrongdoing was taking place when the teacher was taking pictures of my daughter at Dimon elementary school.

The office is neat, well maintained but the minute you don’t agree with her bad directions she holds it against you.

Nothing you can say will she take into consideration and to me that’s not a team.

She wanted me to over exaggerate my income and give her a letter as to when my VA Benefits would increase to 100%.

When I told her the VA doesn’t work like that she scolded me and called me a bully.

My final trial was switched from zoom to inperson and she never contacted me. I showed up at court just me and court clerk who gave me zoom link.  I literally walked from court to her office only for her to reprimand me again.

She quit 2 days before trial only after requesting 2 more additional years (4 total).  48 months of bank statements and taxes I emailed her.  Then She charged me per page then quit 2 days before trial leaving me helpless.

Ms Pitts/Morgan, I wouldn’t recommend her to the last person on earth.

2 days before trial after she got back off a 2 week vacation she ask for these documents.

We wasn’t prepared at all to go to trial because I begged her to have a meeting before her vacation but she said, no we good.

We were never on same page but always went back n forth about things that wasn’t relevant to my case. I.e. witnesses, character statements, sophenas.

I feel like if I am paying you thousands of dollars you should at least honor some of my requests.

“No we not doing that, that’s not a good idea.”

TWO DAYS BEFORE TRIAL SHE QUIT ON ME AND SENT MY TAB FOR ALL THEM PAGES I SENT HER.

UNBELIEVABLE!!!!!
Worst experience ever. ZERO PROFESSIONALISM. HIRE SOMEBODY ELSE
Did their best to help and advise, but wasn't able to help with our exact need. I would recommend this firm to others, based on their willingness to help.
Very helpful and very nice.
You would be better off withdrawing all of your money from the bank and lighting it on fire, at least you will have a moment of warmth.
Beth Pitts is a wonderful lawyer who handled my divorce and a later custody issue in a professional manner.  Would definitely retain her again for any future legal issues.
Beth Morgan is greedy and unethical and I would not recommend you waste your money. And yes.....it is a waste. She will drag out your case and bleed every dime out of you.

Wouldn't recommend this firm to anyone!! Retained Beth Morgan Pitts in 2015 for my divorce. Only married less than a year, one child and no assets. Divorce lasted an entire year with each action taking at least a month to complete. She and her staff were extremely rude and even told me several times that I was not her top priority and my case was at the very bottom of her to-do list! Drug my case out so long that it exhausted my $1500 retainer and charged me an extra $2000 added for all of HER delays and mistakes. Did not provide any information regarding my case for months on end until I finally emailed or left a message. No follow through was ever done with any of the orders and the day she sent my final decree she was done and I was told by her secretary that they would no longer help me.. including with follow through of child support issues etc. Biggest mistake I made was retaining her and air don't recommend.
Very unprofessional as an G.A.L and disrespectful. Luckily I won full physical custody even with her against me.
She’ll take your money and drop you with no explanation, Paid over 2,000 for divorce and at court my case was continued and then she just disappeared……..

She was my first choice when my son was around 4. She handled my case very professionally. My son is now 11 almost 12 and the words of knowledge she gave me came true. She told me how it probably would turn out and low and behold it did. I would definitely recommend her to someone else.

In my experience does not think the rules apply to her - she left me a displaced homemaker and my kids without the basics needed to survive. Not exaggerating -I don't think she likes kids and sees their assets as fair game. Beware! I also got a trafficker vibe off this whole situation. Be careful. I called the Department of Justice and they were a huge help.

Awful experience. She is very unprofessional and was very disrespectful towards me. I am more than willing to share my experience if anyone wants more information because I would hate for anyone else to be subjected to a similar experience, especially when their family is on the line.
Made us pay the consult fee. Talked to us for 20 minutes, recommended we settle it with the prosecution on our own and call back. Did not take our call back. Waste of money. Don't call her

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ Outstanding Experience – Compassionate and Knowledgeable Legal Support

I had the pleasure of working with Attorney Ted Theus and his wife to prepare important legal documents for my mother, including her Will, Power of Attorney (POA), and Medical Directive. What might seem like a simple process to some was completely unfamiliar and overwhelming for me—especially when it involves making decisions for a loved one. I cannot express enough how grateful I am for Mr. Theus’s professionalism, patience, and unwavering support throughout the entire process.

From our first meeting, he took the time to explain everything clearly, step by step, without rushing or assuming I already knew what to do. He ensured that I fully understood each document, and his calm, kind demeanor put my family and me at ease. His patience, empathy, and attention to detail were exactly what we needed during such an important and emotional time.

It’s not every day you find a lawyer who is not only incredibly knowledgeable but also genuinely caring and respectful. Mr. Theus and his wife are true professionals, and I highly recommend them to anyone looking for a lawyer who treats clients with dignity, compassion, and excellence.

Thank you, Attorney Theus & Mrs. Theus, for being a guide, a support, and a trusted advisor when we needed it most.

Thank you,

T. Flewellen

reviews.text: The lady answering the phone is very rude. She would not even let me get a sentence out before she was interrupting me, telling me how she could not help me. If she would have listened, there was a lot more but she likes the sound of her Own voice so much that she would not stop to hear anything I had to say! My mother used this attorney to get a divorce after the divorce. My stepdad went onto my mother’s property and took her life. All I needed to know was how I could get help since my mother did put a warrant out for him and did fall. Stay away order. But she was not interested in helping me. My mother’s life is gone, and her attorney would not even take the time to hear me on anything I had to say after he was supposed to represent her!

I hired Eric B. Funderburk for my custody case, and it is the single worst decision I could have made for myself and my child. His failures in representation have had long-lasting consequences that I am still forced to deal with to this day.

Here are the most serious issues I experienced under his representation:
•	Failure to advise: He did not properly explain the risks or consequences of relocation and custody modification, leaving me without the information I needed to make fully informed decisions.
•	Failure to prepare: He went into court without fully building the factual record or presenting key evidence that was available and necessary to protect my rights.
•	Failure to protect my parental rights: He did not safeguard or preserve my rights under joint legal custody, leaving openings in the order that are now being used against me.
•	Failure to communicate: He repeatedly failed to respond to my concerns in a timely or meaningful way, leaving me without guidance while critical decisions were being made.
•	Failure to follow through: After trial, he never advised me of options for appeal, post-judgment relief, or any corrective measures when it became clear the order was not being enforced.
•	Failure to safeguard long-term interests: He knew I was in a high-conflict custody situation but failed to structure the case or order in a way that would prevent foreseeable issues.

I have been forced to represent myself pro se since 2023 because of how badly I was left positioned. In 2025, when preparing my answer and counterclaim, I reviewed the transcripts and finally saw in black and white the depth of his mistakes. Only then did I realize how badly I had been failed by my own attorney.

When I confronted him about these issues, his only response was that “too much time has passed” and that I should take it up with the Bar Association. No accountability, no acknowledgment, just dismissal.

The truth is this: time does not erase incompetence. Clients trust their attorney to fight for them and protect their interests. Eric B. Funderburk did not do that for me. His neglect left me to clean up the mess years later, alone, while still fighting for my child.

If you are considering this firm, I strongly urge you to look elsewhere. My experience shows that once you leave his office, you are on your own — and when the damage shows itself later, you will get nothing but excuses.

I wanted to thank the office of Eric. B. Funderburk, Attorney at Law, and more specifically Erin (also an Attorney). I live in a different state but received a notice from the local court about a family law matter, that was at first, a little concerning to me.

I was concerned (as anyone might be) because I could not fathom what the court wanted with me,  so I called their office, left a message and was called back by Erin. I told her what I received in the mail and gave her a brief run-down of what the issue could be concerning, she listened intently, asked questions and had me send over the documents I received and she said she would call me after talking with a colleague in her office.

She did in fact call me back and explained that the issue was (more or less) procedural and that I was simply being notified of an action being taken by the court, but not against me; and this was a huge burden lifted off my mind!

Also, though the total time was (I would say) less than a hour, I was not even charged for her work! Though I offered to pay this gesture was appreciated and part of the reason for this long review.

Erin, thank you for taking the time to listen and I appreciate your comforting tone and professionalism during our conversation. I think you mentioned that you are a new attorney and based on our brief interaction I have no doubt you will go very far in your profession. You are a credit to your law office.

If there were more than 5 stars to give for outstanding service then Eric and his crew would get them all. I cannot sing their praises enough for the wonderful job they did when I was failed by 2 other attorneys. With very little time to prepare and a great deal to sift through Eric and his crew rolled up their sleeves and got down to business. The outcome of my case was exactly as I had hoped it would be and I believe that Eric, Josh & Penny were the biggest reason for that. If you are ever in need of an attorney in Alabama I will always recommend this firm. They were all amazing with getting me through the most difficult time of my life.

Dear Ceonna,
Thank you for your kind words! It was a pleasure representing you and we are grateful for the favorable outcome you received. I know the process was long, but the benefit was worth it in the end. Thank you for trusting us enough to recommend our services to others. We truly appreciate it. - Jo Anna

reviews.text:
To anyone in search of an attorney for a custody battle, look no further—this is the only place you need to be. Mrs. Jo Anna is truly the answer to my prayers.

For seven long years, I fought just to be the father my son deserves. I was met with obstacles, frustration, and belittlement at every turn. But the moment I hired Mrs. Jo Anna, everything changed—instantly. I’m not exaggerating when I say that within 20 minutes of retaining her, she was already making moves and taking charge of my case.

To every father who simply wants to be present in their child’s life—trust me, come here. And to the mothers who are exhausted from the constant battles, I can only imagine the miracles she can work for you, too. For years, I longed for someone to see me for what I truly am—a father. Mrs. Jo Anna did just that, and I couldn’t be more grateful.

Mrs. Lynn, I have no words to truly express my appreciation for you. From the very first phone call, you listened to me, understood my frustrations, and made sure I met with Mrs. Jo Anna within 48 hours despite her packed schedule. You kept me informed, handled everything seamlessly, and gave me the reassurance I desperately needed. Your faith in Mrs. Jo Anna only strengthened mine, and I trusted you both wholeheartedly. While Mrs. Jo Anna is lucky to have you, I know for certain that I was the lucky one. My case wouldn’t have been the same without you.

You two are an incredible team—strong, compassionate, and truly dedicated to the people you help. Thank you both for your kindness, support, and unwavering commitment. I owe this office more than I can ever repay. These last seven months have given me the peace I needed to move forward.

Mrs. Jo Anna & Mrs. Lynn, from the bottom of my heart, thank you. You give hope to parents like me, and for that, I am forever grateful.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Dear Troy,
It was our pleasure to represent you during this time of incredible stress and turmoil. Thank you for being such a kind person and most of all, patient with the legal process. It was a true pleasure being your advocate. I am fortunate to have a client as organized as you! Working alongside you was easy! I am ecstatic over the outcome you received and so happy this is just the beginning of many more successes in your future! Please send us updates on that sweet son of yours! Best of luck to you both. - Jo Anna

reviews.text:
I came into Joanna’s Office almost going on two years ago to this day.

Going through a messy and abusive divorce was one of the most challenging and heartbreaking and vulnerable experiences of my life, but Joanna and Mrs. Lynn stood by my side every step of the way. From day one, they believed in me when I felt like no one else did, and their support and determination gave me the strength to keep going. So many times I’d call crying just upset over the entire process confused and wanting to give up myself but they always listened and comforted me when I needed it the most.

Joanna didn’t just handle my case she fought and is still fighting for my safety, dignity, and future for not only I but my three children.  They all worked tirelessly late nights, weekends, and even holidays to make sure that I got the justice I deserved. Their expertise, compassion, and attention to detail were  none like I’ve seen before.

Because of Joanna I was able to break free from my old life and start rebuilding my new life. Her team gave me hope when I thought I had none and is helping me regain control over my future. I will forever be grateful for their dedication and belief not only in me but my entire case, and my case was messy. She started prepping for court right in the middle of mediation but thankfully my case was able to settle without having to step inside a courtroom. Having worked alongside him on numerous cases, I have personally witnessed his unwavering commitment to justice. He approaches every case with professionalism, compassion, and a tireless determination to achieve the best outcome for his clients. His loyalty and work ethic are second to none, ensuring that every client receives the highest level of representation.

Floyd & Floyd is more than just a law firm—it’s a family legacy built on trust, respect, and legal excellence. With his father, Charles Floyd Jr., and soon his son, Chaz Floyd, joining the practice, their tradition of outstanding legal service continues.

Beyond their legal expertise, the entire office staff is exceptional—courteous, efficient, and always willing to assist. Whether you need help with workers’ compensation, criminal defense, or other legal matters, I wholeheartedly recommend Chuck Floyd and his team. When it matters most, they are the law firm you can trust.

Mrs. Grogan is our GAL, and not my lawyer, but I can't imagine her being any different in practicing law. I haven't met the other Grogan, lol, but one Grogan is enough for me!

My name is Rhonda Bowen, I am 62 years old and the following is my review: If you are in need of a kind, understanding, trustworthy, Christian attorney, Mr. Scot Sikes is the right man for the job. I know this firsthand due to my long and drawn out divorce.
It took me over three years to finally realize that divorce was my only realistic option.
During this painful process, I had asked God to lead me to a smart, honest, reliable attorney and one that would speak on my level so I could understand. My heart led me to Mr. Scot Sikes and he represented me.
Mr. Sikes, his wife, Jenny and staff greeted me with love, compassion and genuinely cared for my two sons and I. We were treated with utmost respect and became like family to them. They actually cared about us and what happened to us afterward. I have NEVER known such love, kindness, compassion from a highly intelligent attorney and especially not from his beautiful wife, Jenny.
Just as we turn things over to our Father, God, I had the same confident and peaceful feeling in knowing Mr. Sikes was handling my affairs.
Mr. Sikes made time to answer all my questions and concerns. I felt like I was special because of how much time he spent going over every small detail thus leaving no stone unturned. He worked diligently in providing me with his professional advice in obtaining my futuristic goals.
Mr. Sikes possesses the expertise skills to get the job done correctly, the first time! And in my case, I received Everything I asked for and More!
We became friends and I will ALWAYS have a "Special" place in my heart for Mr. Scot Sikes, Jenny, his wife and Macie, his secretary. I thank God for sending me to this law firm. Through Mr. Sikes divine wisdom and intelligence I was truly Blessed with an abundance from God. Thank you Jesus for working through Mr. Scot Sikes on my behalf.

If any military member is in need of a great lawyer, Scot is your man. I had literally counted myself getting separated from the Army after 17 years of service over a QMP decision, but then I was referred to Scot by a mutual friend, and let me tell you what, this shark of a lawyer reeled me back in and needless to say I am happy to inform you that I will be staying in the Army until my RCP date. Thanks Scot.

Eagerness for combat is a Politicians’ weapon, a Soldier's motivation, and the enemy worst nightmare; Infantry leads the way!

V/R

SFC, USA

reviews.text: I paid them a retainer for a divorce before i was stationed in Korea.  For two years they did nothing but give me a hard time while i was in Korea.   I eventually got a notice about court.   I called them and they wanted a court retainer.  I asked them why and asked for billable hours from my previous retainer.  They declined and then requested to be removed as my counsel due to lack of communication.   Which was a lie.   I always replied to them.  They just wanted more money for nothing. If it's a retainer, they get to keep it, that's why they wanted another one.. reviews.text:
It wont let me do zero stars. but... zero stars.

I hired a lawyer to defend me in a contempt case from this firm.

The lawyer did not seem to care about my issue. It seemed as though they didn't read my emails. They appeared arrogant as if they assumed it would be an easy case despite me telling them that the opposition would bring up anything they could.

At the hearing I sat defenseless as the plantiff and thier counsel presented a mountain of evidence. My counsel didn't ask for an extention or a reccess or anything to review the evidence the opposition brought (this may be because it wouldnt have been allowed). My counsel submitted nothing as evidence.

I asked why their evidence wasn't in the discovery they sent and my counsel told me:  "if it's public domain they do not need to provide it in discovery." Not a great thing to find out as I'm being lied on in court.

When I brought up that the opposition was misleading the court on multiple occassions my counsel shushed me.

When I was on the stand my counsel did not ask anything that seemed to help, I believe this to be because of a lack of knowlege about my parenting plan and of the federal regulations regarding the matter being discussed.

After being found in contempt for something that I was well with in my right to fight, I was ordered to pay the plantiffs legal fees, almost $8k.

After we left the court house my counsel said "well that could have gone worse." No I'm not sure it could have. If I had not even showed up to court it would have been almost the same outcome.

After that my counsel didn't respond to my emails with any kind of immediacy. For months I asked what was going on, they told me nothing had been filed. I asked them to withdraw from my case.

I retained new counsel. My new counsel immediately showed me all sorts of things that opposing counsel had filed with the court.

My new counsel filed a motion to reconsider.

But once found guilty of contempt the judge was unwilling to hear anything in our motion to reconsider about overturning his original decision.

One of the worst experiences of my entire life. The effects of which will be felt for years to come.

I recently contacted Rob Poydasheff to handle a lawsuit for me. It was a “most unusual” case, to say the least. He accepted the challenge and made certain that I had the best understanding of what the outcome could possibly be. It was a LONG SHOT for a win, but Rob never gave up and pushed forward for the best possible result. With Rob’s experience and expertise ----- WE WON!! I will never use anyone other than Rob Poydasheff for any of my legal needs.

reviews.text: We went there to see about getting my dad's bank account released in 2021 to my mom without a will. This lawyer told us it would be over $900.00 to get less than $2000.00 out of the account. We were told to contact the probate judge because this was not right. The Probate Judge let us know this is incorrect and a only a $13.00 small estate letter was needed and this attornoy should have known that. So over $900.00 only cost $13.00. Thanks for charging us a $100.00 consult fee for nothing after telling my mom this was just pocket change and to wait 7 years if we dont want to pay the $900.00 to get the money. PLEASE CONSULT A PROBATE JUDGE BEFORE CONSULTING WITH A LAWYER!!

I picked up my paperwork from them and found a new attorney. Don't waste your time and money with this attorney.  Find someone else that will at least take the time and effort to call you back.

I used Mr. Cross for a matter during the pandemic. I feared that COVID would backlog the case. Fortunately, he got everything done quickly...more quickly than I imagined. I am grateful for the awesome, rapid job.

My husband and I we’re very wrong about this place. In the beginning tensions ran high and we kept giving chance after chance. We were so desperate for them to help us. The paralegal would share stories about her and the many men she was in relationships with. I have every screenshot on my phone. I only ever spoke to the lawyer once during the entire year. If I was upset he would have his mother call. They keep adding to our bill. They finally put us 7 grand in the whole and we already paid them 7 grand. They are a gun for hire. They will take on any case just for money. They will not help you at all. They will give you false hope. Please go somewhere else and hire someone who truly cares about you and your case. This place isn’t it.

"Haste Makes Waste" immediately comes to mind when I think of my experience with Cross Law Firm in Phenix City. I contacted them in behalf of my mom because she as well as myself & other family members did business with their sister company in the past. I didn't feel the need to do research & check reviews as I normally do because we all had pleasant experiences.  Unfortunately, being presumptuous on my part resulted in a waste of time & money for my elderly mom who's on a fixed income. I encourage anyone in advance to please read the reviews & do your own research if you're considering doing business with Cross Law Firm. I truly regret I didn't.
I do not recommend this law firm. Not responsive, poor value and a total waste of time and money.

Talked with Eric (Richard) Cross on multiple occasions. He’s always up front and honest about situations and potential outcomes. Very reasonably priced! I have no complaints and recommend him and his firm!
People who care about you and go above and beyond to make sure your needs are met.

I was hit from behind by a dump truck in 2016.  My original attorney referred me to Litner & Deganian once my case moved to the litigation phase.  From my initial consultation with them they made me feel so comfortable and at ease.  I worked with Dave Rohwedder for my case and he was wonderful.  He was available whenever I needed him to answer any questions.  Everyone in the office made you feel like a member of their family.  I  am so grateful to have had the opportunity to work with them.  They are a special group of people.

I was in a situation where I felt helpless and unsure after a car accident. I attempted to receive care on my own, and ended up at a Chiropractor’s office where a lawyer was recommended to me by the staff. Long story short, that particular lawyer did not even specialize in personal injury. I could not believe that I was stuck with such a lawyer. I expressed my displeasure with a friend who referred me to Litner and Deganian. I’m so glad that I listened to her. I received the care I needed for my injuries. Litner and Deganian made it clear that my care came first and then we would discuss everything else. They checked on me consistently asking me if I was okay and if I needed to receive any other care. They were amazing!

I want to give a special thank you to David T. Rohwedder also known as Dave. He was so professional, personable, and as we say in the south “down to earth”.  He was efficient at his job, but provided comforting words like a friend. I will never forget his kindness, and his skills as a negotiator making sure I received thorough compensation.

My husband, a friend and myself were involved in a head on, roll over collision last December. We were all 3 seriously injured. After we got out of the hospital & rehab several friends & family members urged us to talk to a lawyer, if for nothing more than advice. We had no clue who to call. None of us had had that type experience with a lawyer. It happened that my daughter worked with a lady whose husband was a friend of David Rohwedder & highly recommended him. We gave him a call. He came right out to my house & patiently went through the process with us. We secured his services & he went to work. He was always there if we had a question or concern & took care of the issue right away. He always wanted to know how we were doing & healing. I can’t express how satisfied & pleased we are with his care & the outcome of our case, as well as the friendship he gave to us. We definitely couldn’t have navigated the muddy waters of insurance without him. Thank you, Dave!

Where to begin with Litner & Deganian..........

I was rear ended in a car collision on September 5th 2018. A coworker gave me Steve Litner’s number and praise, so I gave him a call. Within 36 hours of the accident Steve was at my house to see how I was doing and to walk me through the steps he was going to take to make sure all my medical bills were taken care of.  After my initial consult, Steve put Dave Rohwedder on my case, and he’s been my guy ever since.
For the last two years and one month, their entire team has been the best. Dave is extremely knowledgeable when it comes to cases like mine. He always had a great attitude and was willing to help me any way possible. More than that, he always asked me how I was healing. Also, Brittany was on top of all of our communications and keeping me in the loop with things.

Last week my case settled, we did very very well and yesterday my check was cut. Never in my 42 years of life did I think I would be sad to say goodbye to a lawyer. But here we are.

If you are reading this, trust me......you’ve found your legal team.
He will be back!

reviews.text: If I could leave a review with 0 Stars I would but unfortunately I can't. Do not waste your time or energy with mr. Jones or his firm. I don't know much about accidents but my situation seemed pretty much as an open-and-shut case. After pulling out of my driveway I was T-boned by a driver that totaled out the truck I was driving by running a stop sign at a significantly higher speed then posted in my neighborhood which is as well as school zone. After calling the police I was not given a citation I was in fact not even asked for a statement. The officer informed me by looking at the scene the evidence clearly explained what happened. I was late for work so I turned down treatment by the end of the day I was at the ER. I followed up a 2nd time due to pain in my neck back and chest due to my seatbelt. At that time I decided to hire a lawyer. I did not want to get stuck with medical bills. After meeting with mr. Jones The One and Only Time I did he informed me we had a good case. The vehicle was not mine so he did inform the owner he could do as he pleased w/ the insurance company but advised me not to speak w/ the insurance company. The at-fault driver was in a company truck which I was advised not to speak w/the company as well. I was contacted but denied speaking to them. Mr. Jones did give me a doctor which he advised I follow up w/ to ensure nothing major could result from the accident. After several attempts to get in touch w/ the doctor and numerous messages left I never received so much as one call back from the doctor nor mr. Jones. Since I know nothing about accidents I assumed I must not have a case and I would be stuck w/ the medical bills. A few months later I received a certified letter from mr. Jones. Informing me he no longer would be representing me. This review is due to his paralegal calling me not knowing anything about me or my case and when i inform her about the letter she was extremely rude wouldnt answer a single question even about the letter saying quote i know what u want me to say and cut me off w/i have a call ok bye. How unprofessional is this law firm! I should have never called them and you shouldn't either.  In response to the owner my name is C Killingsworth and i gots no time for ya. Crazy head. Didnt you graduate with a law degree. Very Unprofessional. It was your paralegal that called me maybe your staff needs more training or at least should look into the case before making pointless calls either way im not a crazy head  i just told the facts. If u would like to have a respectful conversation and speak like the educated lawyer i assumed u were id be glad to give you a call.

It's been almost two years (starting MAR2017) since my child custody case has not been solved by Mark Jones. As the previous reviews from customers from Google reviews stated, not only Mr. Jones has put my case off on other lawyers that previous had resigned from his firm. I've been calling him since he had sent a letter on a motion to withdraw from my case. I do not understand why he would send such a letter since I have been calling the current lawyer (Mr. Joshua) on the status of my case, in which  I had to find out from the receptionist that he had resigned along with the previous lawyer he assigned me to.

Every time I call to get feedback on why the letter was even sent to me in the first place, his receptionist always keep tell me that "he's in courts all day," and he will get back to me yet I haven't received a return phone call and/or e-mail since I received the letter (NOV2018). I've been calling ever since.

I was trying to work something out with Mr. Jones after he sent me the first letter by responding back to the letter (via telephone call like the letter stated) yet no response from him so I will have to take this issue to the next step and report my issue.

I didn't imagine how my case would be such a prolonged process. This has taken a toll on my daughter and me.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Hi Mr. Grissett: I am sorry you feel like you had a bad experience in your custody case with my firm.

I can't publicly respond to your criticisms of me and my firm under current bar rules (as I read them) since I would have to reveal confidential information.

If you would like me to respond publicly to your review, just let me know, and I will tell you my candid thoughts on you and your case in this public setting, so both sides of the story can be told.

Otherwise, I ask that your remove your review since I can't tell my side of the story without revealing confidential information about you and your case.

I wish you the best, and I'm sorry you and your child's mother are, apparently, not able to resolve this matter to your satisfaction.

reviews.text: Short story: Mr. Morgan got my felony dropped down to a misdemeanor with NO JAIL TIME. Long story: I cant stress enough how well Mr. Morgan  handled my case. It was rocky due to the State being the one pressing charges on me. I was on bond for Felony Terroristic Threats and Acts for about 3 years. Ive since gotten my life together and was having issues getting a good job still with a pending felony charge and all. I contacted Ted’s office as he’s been on my case from the beginning and stated it was best to wait it out, however I needed something done soon. He handled my case with urgency and got in contact with the ADA, and got them to agree to me pleading to a misdemeanor and doing some probation time. After a month tops, we had a court day set and I had a date to make my plea. Once getting to court, he discussed with me how things would go and how to handle myself in front of the judge. After 4 hours of waiting through other cases, it was my turn. Super nervous, but I was confident in Mr. Morgans abilities. After going through normal procedures with the case, the Judge agreed to the terms set forth by Mr. Morgan and the state’s ADA. I AM FELONY FREE, and after a year of pretty relaxed probation, I will be a free man. I can garuntee I’ll never be back in court. But if worst comes to worst, I’ll have Mr. Morgan by my side.

I had the pleasure of working with Ted and Candy at Ted Morgan Law, and I couldn't be more impressed with their professionalism and expertise. From the moment I walked in, I was greeted warmly by Candy and made to feel welcome.

Ted was incredibly knowledgeable and took the time to understand my situation thoroughly. He listened attentively to my concerns and provided clear, concise legal advice.

I would highly recommend Ted Morgan to anyone in need of legal services. Thank you to the entire team for your outstanding work!

They were very compassionate and professional from the first meeting. They helped me navigate both the medical and insurance systems as I dealt with my injuries. They were always available, always willing to help with any issue no matter how trivial, and were always willing to explain matters as they were (not how I might wish they would be). Their assistance, counsel, and friendship were invaluable during the most trying time of my life (due to the accident and numerous other personal tragedies that piled up over the next year). In the end they negotiated a satisfying settlement and made sure every i was dotted and every t crossed. It was very important to me that the entire matter would be handled with the utmost honesty and integrity, and they never disappointed. I would never consider hiring a different attorney and I would always recommend them above all others.

After I was in a car accident, Brian Worstell was recommended to me. I called and was able to meet with him very quickly. I had trouble dealing with insurance alone and having Brian was an immediate help. He was an excellent communicator throughout the entire process, reached out multiple times to see how I was recovering, and gave me multiple updates. He helped relieve the stress of dealing with insurance so that I could focus on recovery. I 100% will be recommending Brian to anyone that needs a personal injury lawyer!
I have the utmost confidence in Brian Worstell! He is a man of Integrity and character! What is personally important to me is that he is a man of faith and a family man!

Mr. Worstell and team were very helpful from start to finish with our case. They really took the time to walk us through from start to finish and were there at any time of day to answer questions we had. I would recommend to anyone who is looking for a family friendly attorney!
We appreciate the timely legal help of Brian Worstell & Ben Philips with a recent legal matter. Communication was great!
It was great having you as my Attorney and if I needed one  again I would call you  and will Recommend you to anyone who needs one  Thank you for everything
Brian Worstell and his staff are very professional as well as personable. He handled a personal injury case for me a couple of years ago with a good result. I highly recommend him.

We could not have been in better hands with Brian Worstell and the entire firm. Every step of the way we were kept informed, given options, and listened to. Communication was fantastic. Hard working, no-nonsense firm that was a calm and experienced voice in a very emotional and turbulent time.

Brian Worstell and the entire team were excellent, providing timely, clear legal expertise and sound advice every step of the way. True professionals who were responsive, knowledgeable, and a pleasure to work with.

If you need a compassionate, professional and thorough team of attorneys by your side, do not hesitate to contact Philips Branch & Hodges Law.
It was so great working with them. They are so professional and so helpful. They get the job done, I highly recommend recommend them and I’m so appreciative of all they done for me.
They are by far (THE VERY BEST LAW FIRM IN COLUMBUS, GA) I would recommend them to anyone who wants to be treated as an individual instead of a PAYCHECK.

I hired this firm( Brian Worstell) to represent me after a slip and fall, and what I didn’t realize at the time was that I wasn’t just hiring attorneys, I was gaining people who truly saw me, valued me, and treated me like family. They fought hard for me every step of the way and stood beside me through multiple surgeries and a season of unimaginable pain. Their support didn’t stop at my case; they showed compassion and care during the personal loss of my only child. I will forever be grateful for their dedication, professionalism, and humanity.
Alisa Lipscomb

Dear Ben,

I am writing to extend my heartfelt gratitude for your exceptional service you provided to my sister and me. There are not enough words to express the support you continually gave us through the whole process of our case.

The case itself was mentally draining for us due to bringing up memories of the events that took place when we lost our loved one. But you were always there to pick us up and continue on. I know you spent countless hours preparing supporting documents and traveling to assure we had all that was needed for the case.

During the duration of the case you kept us well informed of the steps that were about to take place. Your explanations of what would be expected from us were on point and made our confidence stronger to face the upcoming events. We never felt that we had no clue as to what was going to take place or expected of us.

This case was not easy for us but with your support we made it. Some days you had to be more than a lawyer but a shoulder to cry on. Now we consider you part of our family.

Your dedication and support were above and beyond of what I thought would happen.

Thanks to you and your staff for everything that was accomplished with an extradentary ending.

Justice for Mama.

Sincerely
The Girls (DaRell Nix & Denise Dial)

reviews.text:
My name is Rex Williams. I promise on my dead mother's grave that the experience I had with Mr. Gary Bruce is the God honest truth. I was working at Knology of Columbus around the year 2000. I was on the job in the company truck and I was rearended at  stop light by an SUV traveling at an estimated 45 MPH. I was knocked into ongoing traffic, hit by another truck on the passenger side and the front end wrapped around a utility pole. I had a lumber strain shown by x-rays at the time. Mr. Gary Bruce settled the case for $10,000 after almost 2 years! I didn't know anything about law at that time, but I knew the amount was too low. Every time I called to check status or stopped by, he always made me feel ignorant or stupid EVERY SINGLE TIME! Especially when I asked anything about the case! He told me these exact words "my dishonorable discharge from the Army is the reason why the insurance company only settled for $10,000"! I received an honorable discharge from my 1st enlistment which I now receive 100% disability payments each month and full medical coverage due to posttraumatic stress disorder, severe depression, and other physical disabilities. I am a Persian Gulf War veteran of 1990-1991. I began drinking a lot in my 2nd enlist and started experimenting with cocaine because it temporarily suppressed my symptoms. I was discharged because of a positive urine for cocaine in June 1996.
Mr Bruce misrepresented me in the worst way! I had to have a disc fusion at the L4-L5 and L5-S1 in Nov 2016 due to the accident.  I have 2 rods, 6 screws, and 4 cages of bone marrow to replace both disc. Mr Bruce never requested an MRI or anything. He also charged me for every phone call, fax (each page), and every single visit. I received a check for $6000! Not even joking! I later had an accident with a UPS truck in 2018. Because of my experience with Gary Bruce, I no longer trust lawyers and decided to handle the case myself! I never even made a doctor visit and settled for $40,000 and $7500 for the car. Im now a minister of Christ Jesus and I have to let this memory go so this is why I'm sharing my experience. I often wonder if he settled for more and somehow pocketed the money...
reviews.ownerResponse: Rex Williams, We appreciate all feedback and take our client’s feedback very seriously. It is always our goal to provide excellent service. Please contact us at +1 706-786-4780 so that we may properly address your concerns and provide you with a more pleasant experience.

reviews.text: This was my first (and hopefully last) time needing an attorney for a car accident. A friend recommended Gary Bruce Law and I am grateful she did. reviews.text: When my husband was hurt in accident we believed that our insurance and the insurance company who represented the other driver would take care of us.  They basically ignoring us. Brandon Peak and Chris McDaniel  kept us informed about what they needed to do and what was the next step.  During the trial, we were amazed with how professional and passionate they were as they presented our case.  We were very pleased with the results and getting the whole accident resolved. reviews.text: John and I are grateful for Brandon Peak and his dedication to service and detail. Brandon and his team worked tirelessly for over a year to make sure the damages by the responsible party were covered in the most professional and ethical manner. We highly recommend Brandon Peak as one of the most respectable attorneys in Columbus. In addition to their success in litigating cases this firm has an unwavering commitment to their community. Brooke Peak's vision with PWMC Cares to support healthcare workers and first responders in addition to small independent restaurants throughout the Covid pandemic is a testament to their  dedication and support to everyone in this community. Not only did the doors to our restaurant stay open during this time due to the orders coming in from PWMC Cares, but we were also able to give back to those in our neighborhood who were suffering from food insecurity. I applaud Brooke and the firm for this innovative program that provided and is still providing nourishing meals to frontline workers while supporting local restaurants. We are blessed by PWMC's presence in our community!

Brandon Peak is an exceptional attorney who not only exhibits professionalism and expertise in his cases but also shows compassion and kindness to his clients.  We found ourselves in a situation in which the insurance adjuster for the person who had caused damage to our vehicle and, more importantly, to us physically, would not even consider giving us a fair settlement for our medical bills.  We turned to Brandon for help.  He took our case and guided us methodically and easily through the process.   His paralegal, Heather Byrd, worked tirelessly to obtain documents and bills from the hospitals and other medical institutions.  She also responded to our questions in a timely manner, no matter how mundane the question.  Brandon, of course, was simultaneously working diligently to get the insurance adjuster to give us a reasonable settlement.  Throughout the process, he gave us updates on the progress of the case as well as advice if asked.  His expertise in this area is exceptional; the proof is in the extraordinary settlements he gets for his clients.   We heartily recommend Brandon Peak because he definitely gets results for his clients!

I would highly recommend the attorneys at Peak Wooten McDaniel & Colwell LLP, to any friends and family members in need of an outstanding attorney. They are honest, hard-working, ethical lawyers, and are simply the best of the best. I have witnessed firsthand their involvement with our community and it has been amazing!  Their results speak for themselves.

I have communicated with the firm for their services and for them to use my services.  Extremely professional and receptive.  Talented attorneys with a wonderful supportive staff at the perfect location in downtown Columbus.
Such a wonderful group of professionals. Always willing to do the right thing. Their continued support to the community is what attracts me the most to this law firm.
Chris and his team were incredible to work with. Very professional, responsive, knowledgeable, and  responsive through the entire process. Would highly recommend their team to anybody.
Great Honest guys..

It is not often that you need a law firm but when you do you want to make sure you have the right attorney and that is exactly what I got with Peak Wooten McDaniel & Colwell LLP. They were very easy to deal with, friendly while being professional and very invested in getting the right result for me.
I felt they fought for my case as if it were their own and they were very successful!  Chris continued to keep me updated with the progress in a timely manner. It was a pleasure dealing with Chris McDaniel and Joel Wooten.   I would highly recommend them.

In moments of crisis, you want to feel as if you and your loved ones are in trusted hands when seeking justice, and that’s exactly what my experience was with Peak Wooten McDaniel & Colwell.

After my father and son were injured in a car accident, difficulties arose with the neglectful driver’s insurance company. Having never been in such a situation, I did not know where to start to ensure that my family members were supported – but from the beginning to the end Mr. Wooten and Mr. McDaniel continually offered support, kindness, thorough explanations and updates during the process. Not only were they able to assist my son, but after his case was settled, Mr. Wooten and Mr. McDaniel turned to my father to assist him as well.

The kindness the firm showed throughout meant the world to me, and their patience with my father and willingness to answer my myriad questions is something for which I will always be grateful. During life’s scary moments you want to know that someone is looking out for your family’s best interests, and I can firmly say that Peak Wooten McDaniel & Colwell did just that.

We met Chris McDaniel and Brandon Peak directly following a most horrific fatal crash that left two-year-old twin children without their dad, and the remaining family in shock.

As we moved through the process, discussions we had were met with sincere and heart-felt direction from Mr. McDaniel, regardless of how many times we asked the same questions. We always had immediate response from Mr. McDaniel, day, or night, to address concerns and ease our minds.

Thank you, for taking such diligent care with all of us through this tragedy. I recommend this group of professionals to anyone facing the same. Now considered part of the family, hats off to you and your team!

Brandon Peak represented me a few years ago when I was involved in a car accident.  I was hit by another driver.  Brandon & his paralegal Marci Martinez, did a wonderful job handing my case & my needs.  They handled everything from the minute I hired them, until it was resolved.  They treated me with kindness, respect & had so much compassion for me.  They were there for me every time I called them.  I highly recommend PWMC law firm to my family, friends, church members & work family.  Brandon Peak is an excellent attorney & shows compassion & he is always there to help.  Thank you Brandon Peak & Marci for everything.  If I could give you 10 stars, I would!  Love, Clare Willis

Excellent attorneys! I have known and worked with Joel, Brandon and Joseph for years. I consider them dear friends. Their new firm will work tirelessly for their clients, and they will demand the best result. They are passionate about helping the community outside of work through philanthropic and other initiatives. These gentlemen are also always happy to provide mentorship to fellow attorneys (young and old!). They are well respected near and far, and I highly recommend this Firm to anyone looking for top tier legal advice.

Brandon Peak is intelligent, clever, and experienced in handling the most serious and complex litigation cases. Brandon clearly appreciates and deeply honors the responsibility to guide families after a serious injury or death.  His moral compass ensures that he does right by families who have experienced catastrophic loss and put their trust in him to obtain the right result.
The well-established Peak Wooten McDaniel & Colwell team has the expertise, resources and willingness to try complex cases, and they obtain the highest possible settlement value for their clients.

I cannot speak from personal experience in regard to this firm's ability to practice law. One can read the rave reviews left by others that clearly demonstrate their excellence in that arena. However, I can attest to the overwhelming amount of time, money, and support this firm has dedicated to the healthcare workers of their community during the ongoing battle against COVID19. When the first waves of the pandemic washed over the country, it seemed as though everyone had a new-found appreciation for services provided and sacrifices made by healthcare workers. People banged pots and pans from their balconies; they parked in empty visitor parking lots with their lights flashing; they sent care packages and food by the truckloads. But, as the tide ebbed, so did the support, even though each wave was bigger than the last:  EXCEPT in the case of PWMC. When everyone else faded into the new normal, PWMC continued to show up; they continued to check-in; they continued to offer tokens, both large and small, of their heartfelt appreciation for the ongoing work taking place within the walls of their local hospitals. And after two years, they've showed no sign of slowing down or backing off. If their dedication to their clients and their cases is anything like their dedication to their community, then one would be hard-pressed to find better representation. Thank you for all you do, PWMC.

I associated Brandon Peak and his team to help me bring justice to two boys whose father was killed in a terrible accident.  This was a complex premises liability case involving multinational corporations.  Brandon Peak was always three steps ahead of these defendants.  When they say they are "exceptional trial lawyers," this is not just a slogan but it is a way of life.

Going through a tragic loss is unbearable then add a lawsuit on top of it is just too much. The first thing Brandon told me was he was here for me and he would handle the heavy lifting, for me to worry my children and their grief. And that’s exactly what he did. Brandon and Marci went above and beyond and always made me feel like I was a top priority. They took on my case and treated me as if I were their family.

When I first needed a lawyer I found Mr. Andrew Dodgen. I worked with him for about 2½-3 years. Im now working with his son Mr. Ben Dodgen and I couldn't be happier. Dogden law firm does not deserve this one star rating. I wasted $6000 with Andrew and Benjamin Dodgen. And Yes I include both because both billed me for charges. I hired Andrew Dodgen in 2023  for an contempt charge and modification. I was never seen before the Judge to address my modification case. The only time I was before a judge was due to 2 contempts filed by my ex. I gave Andrew Dodgen all the evidence to prove these contempts were based on false allegations. He presented nothing to the judge. He just sat there like a log on a bench. Their paralegal told me in person that she didn't have to return my calls. I called often because my case was going nowhere. Everytime I tried to speak with the paralegal, she was " tied up in evidenary hearing". My court date was canceled because no one advised  the guardian ad litem to look at the case. The Judge canceled the case days prior to trial because he didn't have the guardian ad litem report. Once he canceled it, A. Dodgen reached out to the guardian ad litem. Why was this being done at the last minute? The judge said an guardian ad liem was need during court date in July 2024. So why wasn't she notified before our next October court date. Push forward 2025, A. Dodgen decides to become a full time judge. He informs me of this in June 2025, and sets my mediation for July 28, 2025. On August 1, 2025 A. Dodgen left the practice to be a judge. I was told if I give them more money I could retain the son Ben Dodgen. Many reviews here says Ben is a great lawyer. He offered help to fix the mistakes made during my contract. Yes he was willing to recommend other attorneys who could take my case. But why should I chose another lawyer in Columbus. Lawyers in Columbus are preers. So beware of Columbus lawyers because they will turn around and testify for opposing counsel after they sever ties with you case. Yes I know they're no longer my attorney but when you failed to properly advise a client at mediation of what needs to be included in the final documents. I went to mediation with a list of what I wanted to be addressed. I showed this to A.Dodgen and he told me I could get child support because my visitation time was being increased. But they failed to put it in writing before mediation ended. By being naive of the process,  I thought child support would be automatic. After Ben allowed the opposing counsel to type up the final order and recommendations, the order became one sided. It stated  every thing I would do in carrying the insurance for the kids etc. But it stated nothing about the defendant. The legal way of avoiding conversation or change in child support. They put in the document all other things will be the same. So you just lost support with that one statement. If I was fully advised by my counsel I would not have signed the mediation agreement. So the opposing cancel files a motion to compel me to sign this document. I'm refusing because I'm stuck with all the kids bills and my counsel didn't tell me this during mediation. When I enter Pro se, because I have been  drained financially paying Dodgen, mediator and guardian at liem on a VA disability income. To my surprise at trial, Benjamin Dodgen shows up as the opposing counsel witness. He states oh, she was satisfied with the final mediation agreement.. Andrew or Benjamin didn't explain to me that by signing this document,  I forfeited receiving child support. I thought child support was considered in every case automatically. They didn't tell me I needed to request it in the mediation document. Benjamin even sat next to my opposing counselor in the lawyer's box
Do not hire attorneys in Columbus for your case. Get an outside attorney who is not apart of Columbus' good old boy system. I as a client didn't know what needed to be put in a mediation, a judge and lawyer should know what needs to be stated in a mediation agreement. Well this judge and lawyer didn't explain to me what I was signing. Now I'm stuck with no child support and Benjamin  Dodgen testifying for my opposing counsel. This is as shady as it gets.

Then he said “send me this document and we’ll file the complaint once we get it.” I sent it over, and the complaint was never filed. I still hadn’t received the agreement. I spent a month back and forth with him on the phone and via email, trying to at least get something. He scheduled a call with me and his dad, only to have his dad bail on the meeting and leave me to reschedule. I had already taken time off work for that call and I did everything I was supposed to do.
A week later I asked for a status update on my case, and he wouldn’t give me any sort of timeline. So we went from “send me this to file the complaint” to “I can’t give you any estimate on when it will be filed.” Then he recommended I find another attorney.
He did not follow through on a single thing he told me was he going to do, and I had to find another lawyer and start over from the beginning after having wasted a month of my limited time required to file. It was unprofessional in every respect.

I retained and fully payed Mr. Kennedy for a bond hearing on 2 cases a li bit over a month ago.  His secretary told me if I hire them,Mr. Kennedy will visit my loved onefew days before the court date. Fast forward one month later I realized that he had never called or emailed me as far as what the plan is. I called his office from Monday to Friday a week before the court date and he never returned my calls. I emailed him expressing my concerns he never called. The day before the court date I called his office again and told his assistant to make sure that we are on for the case tomorrow and she told me yes. And I told her I would be driving two hours to be here so I just wanted to make sure that he will be appearing in front of the judge tomorrow before driving. On the day of the court case I went to court and I found out that Mr. Kennedy was not in court and my family member was not on the list. I called his office after I left the clerks office and his assistant told me he is in the meeting with somebody. The clerk’s office told me he has not entered in on the case yet which he could have told me if he picked my calls or responded to my emails. In the emails I let him know that my husband was the one who referred me to him. I am saying that because a month ago he did not have all five stars. I believe he had the other reviews deleted. I could have saved my $2500 and waited till 90 days for the judge to grant us a bond with no lawyer. He is worse than a public defender.

reviews.text: Cash out refinance mortgage handled by Mr. Stutzman. Five creditors were to be paid and I was charged $295.00 for overnight fees. This was on August 30, 2024. Three days later none were paid. I called the office and was informed that the payments could not be made overnight to P. O. Boxes. Which is not true if you do express mail through United States Postal Service and do a waiver of signature. Fed Ex can not do overnight to P. O. Boxes. I reasoned that since the overnight service was not done that I should get the $295.00 refunded to me. This law form would not and said that is just what we charge. Only two of the five creditors have been paid through regular mail as of September 11,2024. These were supposed to be pay offs as of September 3, 2024. I would not recommend this law firm as they literally stole $295.00 from me for a service that was not done. Once they got their money they did not care that I was dissatisfied with their service.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Hi Mrs. Sellers,

We're really sorry to hear about the trouble you've experienced with the refinance process and the unsecured creditor payoff fees; these were never intended to be overnight fees. I believe that we have spoken to you about these standard payoff fees for your creditors, and it is also stated on your ALTA closing statement as “payoff services - unsecured”, but please reach out to us if you have additional questions. Thank you for your time and for trusting us with your closing.

reviews.text: Completed a closing for house purchase on 2/14/24.  The accounting practices left much to be desired. Keep in mind that wire transfer was transmitted and confirmed cleared by the distributing institution (2) days before closing.  Since the 2/14/24 closing date, my RE agent has been given the run around and has yet to receive the buyer's fee as of the date of this review.  That's just bad business practices. reviews.text: They totally sabotaged my case. It's a disgrace what they did to a disabled veterans SSDI claim. I feel as if they wanted me to fail.
reviews.ownerResponse:
We take accusations of this nature very seriously, and we respectfully disagree with your characterization of our firm’s conduct.

Because your review does not include identifying information that allows us to confirm you were ever a client of our firm, we are unable to address the specifics of your claim. That said, the allegation that our firm would intentionally “sabotage” a disability case is simply inconsistent with both our business model and our professional ethics.

Brock & Stout represents Social Security disability claimants on a contingency-fee basis. We are not paid unless benefits are awarded. Quite simply, there is no financial, professional, or ethical incentive for our firm to want a client’s claim to fail. Our success is directly tied to the success of the people we represent.

For nearly three decades, our firm has focused on providing diligent, knowledgeable representation to injured and disabled individuals, including veterans. Our attorneys and staff take pride in advocating for clients at every stage of the disability process and in keeping clients informed about the challenges and realities of these cases, which can be lengthy and complex.

We have the utmost respect for those who have served our country. Any suggestion that we would intentionally work against a veteran’s interests is particularly troubling and does not reflect who we are or how we practice law.

We encourage any former client with concerns about their case to contact our office directly so we can review the matter and address those concerns appropriately. We remain committed to professionalism, transparency, and doing everything ethically and legally possible to pursue benefits on behalf of our clients.

Brock & Stout Attorneys at Law

reviews.text: Mr. May always treats me so kind with thoughtfulness and respect whenever I come in. Love working with him on any legal issues I need assistance with.

Life-Changing Representation — Grateful Beyond Words

Working with Jorja during our step-parent adoption case was nothing short of incredible. From the very first consultation, she and her staff showed not only a deep knowledge of Alabama law, but also compassion, patience, and fierce dedication to protecting the best interests of our family.

She guided us through a highly contested and emotionally complex situation — with out-of-state custody history, jurisdictional questions, and an opposing party who was anything but cooperative. Despite all that, she stayed calm, focused, and strategic. She handled every curveball with professionalism and confidence, and when the time came, she presented our case clearly and powerfully in court.

Because of her, our adoption was granted and our children had the voice that they deserve.

She's not just an attorney; she’s a fighter, an advocate, and someone who truly cares. I will never stop being thankful for what she helped us accomplish!

Thank you again!!!

Let me begin this post with the most sincere thank you to Jorja Loftin and staff.  In February of 2020, my spouse and I decided to adopt a baby who was born to parents who were unable to keep her.  Due to the pandemic and other attorney’s who were incapable of following through with the adoption, we were unable to complete the process. I was advised to contact Jorja to discuss our issues.  When I called her office, her staff was very responsive to my needs and concerns. Jorja returned my call promptly and was so kind, compassionate and interested in my situation!  She immediately offered her services, removed all stress and anxiety that we had endured for a year and began the adoption process.  What others attorney’s could not even begin, Jorja completed in just a little over a month!!!  Our baby girl is now legally adopted and our lives are complete and we are so grateful.  If you are ever in need of a fantastic attorney who will exceed your expectations....please give her a call.  It will be the best decision you make today!!!
Sincerely,
Kimberly Moulton
